article_meta.title: Jurisprudence People Are Taking the Wrong Lesson From This Weekend's Dual Massacres By Ido Kirson and Austin Sarat Dec 1

reasoning: Solid avoidance of quoted text and some correct flags of author-level spin/emotion, but includes at least one dubious 'Bias by Omission' flag and mislabels at least one instance. Misses several clear, salient bias examples in the authors' prose.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged passages come from the authors’ own prose; no quotes or attributed source language were flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: One clear overreach: labeling the Bondi sentence as 'Bias by Omission' for lacking perpetrator/method details; another item (the 'wrong question' segment) is arguable but not clearly wrong.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed multiple strong instances: 'Surely, we will hear...' (Mind Reading), 'our current pro-gun Supreme Court' (Subjective Qualifying/Word Choice), 'It is clear that avenues exist...' (Opinion as Fact), and 'After last weekend, it will only get worse.' (Unsubstantiated/Opinion as Fact).
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Emotional language correctly labeled; 'reasonable gun regulations help...' appropriately tagged as Opinion-as-Fact. However, 'That's the wrong question... History tells us...' is better labeled as Spin/Slant, and the 'Bias by Omission' tag is misapplied.
